blocks.content: Shironé was so focused on the teacher's face that it felt like he was drilling holes into it, making his current demeanor unfamiliar.

blocks.speaker: Teacher
blocks.content: Is school life difficult for you?

blocks.speaker: Shironé
blocks.content: No, it's fun. I enjoy studying.

blocks.speaker: Teacher
blocks.content: Why didn't you say anything? I heard the kids were bullying you.
Shironé had already guessed as much. In fact, it would have been strange if he hadn't known. Lately, the children had been openly tormenting him.

blocks.speaker: Shironé
blocks.content: It's okay. You don't need to worry.
Siena, who had heard detailed accounts of the children's misbehavior from Maria, didn't believe Shironé's words.

blocks.speaker: Siena
blocks.content: You can be honest about these things. There's no shame in it.

blocks.speaker: Shironé
blocks.content: I'm really fine. It's disappointing that I haven't made friends, but if they dislike me, there's nothing I can do about it.

blocks.speaker: Siena
blocks.content: Excluding a student is a bad thing.

blocks.speaker: Shironé
blocks.content: You can't force feelings. Of course, it was hard at first, but I'm okay now. If the kids in Class Seven dislike me, so be it. It doesn't mean the whole world hates me.
Siena understood. For some, the group they belonged to was their entire world, but for those who looked beyond, such groups were merely temporary waypoints.

blocks.speaker: Shironé
blocks.content: I want to meet more people. I believe there are people out there who will take my story seriously.
Shironé was clear about his stance on early advancement. He believed that in Class Five or above, he would find friends who would accept him without any sense of inferiority.

blocks.speaker: Siena
blocks.content: Shironé, I've heard your resolve clearly. I'll take it into consideration at the faculty meeting.

blocks.speaker: Siena
blocks.content: With that, I conclude my remarks on Shironé's early advancement.
In a meeting attended by Principal Alpheus and senior class teachers, Siena submitted documents related to the Class Seven bullying incident and took her seat.
The teachers frowned as they reviewed the contents.
The details revealed how persistently Shironé had been excluded, with the pretext that he couldn't use magic.

blocks.speaker: Teacher
blocks.content: Does this make any sense? Bullying a classmate for such a trivial reason?

blocks.speaker: Teacher
blocks.content: Such things are common in lower classes. There are many young children, and some students stay behind despite their age due to lack of talent. The classes themselves lack distinction.

blocks.speaker: Teacher
blocks.content: Then it would be best to advance Shironé quickly. He could graduate within four years. With neighboring schools challenging Alpheas Magic School, the more early graduates, the better.

The age of graduates was one of the metrics proving a school's excellence. Even if they didn't excel in society, the reputation would suffer, but rumors were circulating that other schools were competitively producing early graduates.
It was a standard tactic to elevate a school's prestige. Once the prestigious title was secured, talented individuals would flock, and only then could the quality of education be improved.

In the face of fierce competition from other schools, Alpheas Magic School's strategy was to produce not only early graduates but also qualitatively outstanding students. In that sense, the teachers had high expectations for Shironé.

blocks.speaker: Sad
blocks.content: I'm somewhat opposed.
Sad raised his hand and cast a dissenting vote.
Siena's expression twisted.
It was infuriating to hear objections when early advancement was practically a done deal.
But Alpheus showed interest in the first opposing opinion.

blocks.speaker: Alpheus
blocks.content: You're opposed. Why is that?

blocks.speaker: Sad
blocks.content: I know Shironé is talented. But the core of this incident is that Shironé can't use magic. The children's backlash stems from that. Shouldn't we address that clearly?

blocks.speaker: Siena
blocks.content: I've already mentioned this. Shironé has been learning general knowledge and hasn't had the chance to focus on a single discipline. Personally, I'm confident this is the optimal path for his growth.

blocks.speaker: Sad
blocks.content: I'm not questioning the method. The problem is that you're favoring Shironé too much, Teacher Siena.

blocks.speaker: Siena
blocks.content: Excuse me?

blocks.speaker: Sad
blocks.content: Regardless, the kids in Class Seven are also studying hard after entering the school. Whether Shironé had time to acquire knowledge or not, that should also be part of the evaluation criteria. Otherwise, there's no reason not to advance other students early, even if their Spirit Zone is weaker than Shironé's, since they're theoretically superior.

blocks.speaker: Siena
blocks.content: Don't spout nonsense! I didn't judge this matter emotionally. My conclusion is based strictly on school rules. The main evaluation criterion for lower classes is clearly the Spirit Zone.

blocks.speaker: Sad
blocks.content: The problem is the students' backlash. This incident could demotivate them. Teachers aren't here to pour everything into one genius, are they?

blocks.speaker: Siena
blocks.content: It's not a teacher's job to neglect a talented student because of the children's misplaced jealousy!
The two simultaneously slammed the table and stood up.
It was a familiar spectacle whenever there was a meeting.
